{"society_power": 5.5, "state_power": 6.5, "society_change": 0.5, "state_change": -0.5, "key_event": "Nitrate-era labor mobilization met by state massacres", "reasoning": "Rising nitrate-camp and port labor organizing (strikes, mutual-aid societies, working-class press) strengthened civil society despite brutal repression at Iquique and the Semana Roja, while the oligarchy-captured Parliamentary Republic left the state fiscally rich but institutionally fragmented and dependent on foreign capital, eroding its coherent autonomous capacity."}
